content: Never ship LLM API keys in an extension — bundle is inspectable. Standard pattern: extension calls your backend with the user's auth token; backend holds provider keys, applies per-user rate limits/quotas, validates and shapes prompts server-side (methodology prompt lives on the server, also protecting the IP), logs usage for cost tracking. Gateways like LiteLLM exist but a thin custom proxy endpoint is typical for a single-provider SaaS.
